# verifylog — FPGA / register-transfer verification artifacts

Parent: [Gnosis README](../README.md)

This directory holds **machine-generated or curated outputs** from RTL simulation, formal checks, and theorem-witness exports for the Gnosis **synthesis bridge** (`rtl-bridge/`). It is **not** source code; most subfolders are gitignored except README stubs and reference snapshots.

## Getting Started

- **What:** generated or curated RTL simulation, formal-check, and theorem-witness artifacts for the synthesis bridge.
- **Why:** it gives hardware-facing work a place to store small reference evidence without pretending large waveforms belong in git.
- **How:** regenerate artifacts through `rtl-bridge` commands where possible, and commit only small reference snapshots that lock regressions.
- **Next:** read the naming note so RTL hardware artifacts do not get confused with RTL-SDR radio work.

**Scope:** v0 centers on a **bounded resolution-lift FSM** (real admission / control logic), with paths to grow into full mesh-adjacent RTL. It was never meant to mean “FPGA work stops at telemetry.”

## Naming (read this once)

- **Register-transfer level (RTL)** here means **VHDL / Verilog / formal logs** for FPGA and silicon workflows.
- **RTL-SDR** elsewhere in this repo means **software-defined radio** (see [`../rtlsdr-mock-sim/README.md`](../rtlsdr-mock-sim/README.md)). The acronyms collide; use full phrases in new docs.

## Layout

| Path | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `vhdl/` | Generated or hand-checked VHDL (e.g. `gnosis_resolution_lift_fsm_v0.vhd`). Prefer regenerating from `rtl-bridge` rather than editing without updating the emitter. |
| `traces/` | Simulation waveforms (VCD/FST), GHDL logs, transcript copies. |
| `formal/` | SymbiYosys / SMTBMC logs, `.sby` copies, PASS/FAIL summaries. |
| `witnesses/` | JSON linking Lean theorem names to bounded IR parameters (see `witnesses/*.json`). |
| `ref-runs/` | Optional checked-in **green** reference bundles (small) for CI comparison when tools are absent locally. |

## Green criteria (v0)

1. Golden vectors from `rtl-bridge` match the Rust reference (`distributed_inference::attention_closure_lift`) bit-for-bit on lift action and post-lift `observer_resolution`.
2. When `GHDL` is available and `GNOSIS_RTL_TOOLS=1`, `pnpm run validate:rtl-sim` passes synthesis of the testbench + entity.
3. Formal targets are **optional** (`validate:rtl-formal`); archive outputs here when run.

## Retention

Large waveforms and vendor reports should stay **local** or in CI artifacts; only commit **small** reference logs under `ref-runs/` when they encode a regression lock.
